name: Río Turones, Fuerte de la Concepción e Ponte Romana Malpartida — circular por ele Zona de Proteção Especial do Douro Internacional e Vale do Rio Águeda
region_intro: Road cycling around Figueira De Castelo Rodrigo offers varied terrain, from rolling hills to challenging ascents through natural environments. The region is characterized by its diverse landscapes, including pine forests and the river valleys of the Côa and Douro. Cyclists can expect routes with varied elevation profiles, providing options for different skill levels. The area's geography is shaped by inland hills and features like the Serra da Marofa, which reaches 977 meters. Best road cycling routes around Figueira De Castelo Rodrigo The most popular road cycling route is Río Turones, Fuerte de la Concepción e Ponte Romana Malpartida — circular por ele Zona de Proteção Especial do Douro Internacional e Vale do Rio Águeda, a 46.0 miles (74.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route features moderate climbs through the Douro International Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castelo Rodrigo Village – Almeida Castle loop from Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo, a difficult 47.0 miles (75.7 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and passes through historical villages. Local road cyclists also love the End of N222 in Almendra – Steep terrain in the Côa Valley loop from Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo, a 58.2 miles (93.7 km) trail leading through the Côa Valley, often completed in about 4 hours 42 minutes. Road cycling around Figueira De Castelo Rodrigo is defined by rolling hills, pine forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Figueira De Castelo Rodrigo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes vary in difficulty and length, providing options for different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo?
A: The region is particularly picturesque in early spring, specifically February and March, when the almond trees blossom, creating a vibrant display, especially around Barca d'Alva. Summer also offers a beautiful landscape with orange hues. The varied terrain means cycling is enjoyable across multiple seasons, depending on your preference for scenery and temperature.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in this area?
A: Road cycling around Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo features varied terrain, from rolling hills and pine forests to challenging ascents through natural environments and inland hills. You'll encounter diverse elevation profiles, including gentle gradients and more demanding climbs, with the Serra da Marofa reaching 977 meters.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for various abilities. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mata de Lobos loop from Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 30.4 km trail with moderate elevation gain.

Q: Which routes offer views of the Douro River or Côa Valley?
A: You can experience the stunning landscapes of the Douro River and Côa Valley on several routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1405823371/rio-turones-fuerte-de-la-concepcion-e-ponte-romana-malpartida-circular-por-ele-zona-de-protecao-especial-do-douro-internacional-e-vale-do-rio-agueda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Turones, Fuerte de la Concepción e Ponte Romana Malpartida — circular por ele Zona de Proteção Especial do Douro Internacional e Vale do Rio Águeda&lt;/a&gt; route features moderate climbs through the Douro International Natural Park. For the Côa Valley, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32132500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of N222 in Almendra – Steep terrain in the Côa Valley loop from Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging ride through this significant natural area.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or villages to explore along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo Rodrigo Village – Almeida Castle loop from Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt; passes through historical villages, including the award-winning Historical Village of Castelo Rodrigo, where you can see the castle ruins and other ancient structures. The region is also part of the GR22 – Great Route of the Historical Villages of Portugal.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints accessible by road bike?
A: The region offers several breathtaking viewpoints. The Serra da Marofa, reaching 977 meters, provides expansive vistas. Other notable viewpoints include Alto da Sapinha and Santo André das Arribas, all offering stunning panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes, including vineyards and forests.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1405823371/rio-turones-fuerte-de-la-concepcion-e-ponte-romana-malpartida-circular-por-ele-zona-de-protecao-especial-do-douro-internacional-e-vale-do-rio-agueda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Turones, Fuerte de la Concepción e Ponte Romana Malpartida — circular por ele Zona de Proteção Especial do Douro Internacional e Vale do Rio Águeda&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32128925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidadelhe – Steep terrain in the Côa Valley loop from Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What attractions can I visit near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, you can explore several attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3886376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo Rodrigo Discovery Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5282884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barca d'Alva&lt;/a&gt;, located by the Douro River, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5282815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Agostinho da Silva in Barca d’Alva&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3569260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Hierro&lt;/a&gt; offers another interesting point of interest.

Q: Are there any protected natural areas I can cycle through or near?
A: Yes, the region is home to significant natural features. You can cycle through or near the Douro International Natural Park, as highlighted by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1405823371/rio-turones-fuerte-de-la-concepcion-e-ponte-romana-malpartida-circular-por-ele-zona-de-protecao-especial-do-douro-internacional-e-vale-do-rio-agueda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Turones, Fuerte de la Concepción e Ponte Romana Malpartida — circular por ele Zona de Proteção Especial do Douro Internacional e Vale do Rio Águeda&lt;/a&gt; route. The Faia Brava Reserve, Portugal's only protected Nature Reserve, is also in the vicinity, offering unique opportunities for nature observation.

Q: What makes Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo a good destination for road cycling?
A: Figueira de Castelo Rodrigo offers a captivating blend of challenging and scenic road cycling routes, diverse and picturesque natural landscapes, and significant historical sites. The area provides mostly well-paved, quiet roads with varied elevation profiles, making it an ideal destination for outdoor enthusiasts seeking both physical challenge and cultural immersion.
tips: This is the border between Spain and Portugal, where the town of Escarigo is located near the Turones River. The road that crosses the Turones River shows a green and close area of life!

Armed conflicts between borders have been the daily bread in these areas between the two countries. It was built in the 17th century and currently has more than three centuries of history, where its walls are still standing. Currently, it is rehabilitated and is a hotel.

Beautiful Roman bridge that is located next to the town of Malpartida and where the bridge avoids the Ribeira das Alvercas. Right next to this old bridge is a water fountain where you can stop to refill your bottles.
